meta.note: 6 complete, standalone introduction paragraphs per question. No template blanks — each string is finished, publishable text, contextualised using that question's own topic and writingPoints. Keyed by question id (matches essay-questions.json). galleries.html Introduction mode should fetch this file and, for the selected question, display intros[0..5] as 2 pages of 3 cards — no runtime text generation needed for this mode.

intros: It is a well-established fact that society has changed almost beyond recognition since the early twentieth century, as advances in medicine, technology and living standards have transformed daily life. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on whether these changes have genuinely made life better.

These days, it is easy to take modern comforts for granted, given that people a century ago faced far harsher conditions with little access to healthcare or convenience. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether life today truly represents an improvement.

It is generally assumed that modern life is easier than it was a hundred years ago, since so many of the hardships our ancestors faced have now been overcome. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on how far this assumption actually holds true.

It is common knowledge that the pace of change over the past century has been extraordinary, considering that inventions such as antibiotics, the internet and modern transport did not even exist a hundred years ago. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether all this progress has made us better off.

Over the past century, there has been a dramatic transformation in almost every area of daily life, as healthcare, entertainment and communication have all advanced beyond what earlier generations could have imagined. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on whether this transformation amounts to genuine progress.

It is generally assumed that nobody would choose to live exactly as people did a hundred years ago, as modern medicine, technology and living conditions have improved so significantly since then. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on whether that assumption is actually justified.

These days, many people spend the majority of their waking hours at work, although not everyone agrees on what makes a job worth doing. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of prioritising salary over job satisfaction.

Nowadays, choosing a career often means weighing up financial reward against personal fulfilment, in spite of the fact that few jobs offer both in equal measure. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of earning well versus enjoying your work.

Few people would deny that a high salary makes life more comfortable, despite the fact that spending long hours in an unfulfilling job can take a serious toll on wellbeing. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of choosing money over job satisfaction.

It is widely recognised that the type of work someone does affects far more than their bank balance, although salary remains the deciding factor for many when choosing a career. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of putting money before enjoyment at work.

It is generally assumed that a well-paid job is worth any amount of stress, despite the fact that poor job satisfaction is closely linked to mental health problems. Consequently, it is essential that we study the arguments in favour and against choosing income over enjoyment.

Few people would deny that we spend a huge proportion of our lives at work, in spite of the fact that not everyone can find a job that pays well and feels rewarding. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of prioritising money over job satisfaction.

It is a well-established fact that species such as tigers, rhinos and elephants are disappearing at an alarming rate due to hunting and habitat loss. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on whether we should do everything possible to save them.

These days, hardly a week goes by without a news story about another animal species edging closer to extinction. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on how far we should go to protect endangered wildlife.

It is generally assumed that protecting endangered animals is simply a matter of goodwill, when in fact their survival is closely tied to the balance of entire ecosystems. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on whether maximum conservation effort is justified.

It is common knowledge that human activity is the main driver of animal extinction, considering that habitat destruction and illegal hunting continue largely unchecked. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether we owe it to these species to protect them at any cost.

Over the past few decades, the number of animals classified as endangered has risen sharply across almost every continent. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on whether saving them should be treated as an urgent priority.

It is generally assumed that losing a single species has little effect on the wider world, as its role often goes unnoticed until it is gone. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on why I believe this assumption is mistaken.

These days, people often build close relationships with friends they choose for themselves, although family ties are usually formed without any choice at all. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of prioritising friendship over family.

Nowadays, many young people feel closer to their friends than to their relatives, in spite of the fact that family is often the first to help in a crisis. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of valuing friends above family.

Few people would deny that friendships bring enjoyment and shared interests into our lives, despite the fact that family relationships tend to prove more dependable over time. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of ranking one above the other.

It is widely recognised that the relationships we value most tend to shift as we move through different stages of life, although many people cling to one type of relationship as more important throughout. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of prioritising friends or family.

It is generally assumed that family will always come first, despite the fact that many people rely far more heavily on friends for day-to-day support and companionship. Consequently, it is essential that we study the arguments in favour and against each type of relationship.

Few people would deny that both friends and family shape who we become, in spite of the fact that most of us instinctively rank one above the other. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of treating friendship and family as equally important.

There is a growing recognition that vehicle emissions, industrial waste and plastic are pushing the planet's ecosystems to breaking point, in order to protect the environment for future generations. Consequently, this essay will suggest ways to address the world's most pressing pollution problems.

It is a well-established fact that our rivers, seas and cities are becoming increasingly contaminated, in order to sustain the lifestyles that modern societies have grown used to. Consequently, this essay will suggest ways to address these worsening pollution problems.

It is often argued that pollution has become too widespread to control, as a result of decades of unchecked industrial growth and rising consumption. Consequently, this essay will suggest ways to address whether these problems can realistically be solved.

It is often argued that transport and industry are the main sources of global pollution, due to the fact that both continue to rely heavily on fossil fuels. Hence, it is vital to explore practical solutions to reduce their environmental impact.

It is a well-established fact that plastic waste and toxic emissions are damaging oceans and the atmosphere alike, due to the fact that regulation has struggled to keep pace with industrial output. Therefore, it is essential to find workable remedies for these urgent environmental problems.

There is a growing recognition that pollution poses one of the greatest threats to the planet's future, owing to the continued dependence on cars, factories and fossil fuels. Hence, it is vital to explore practical solutions such as renewable energy and stricter regulation.

It is a well-established fact that the fashion industry has an enormous influence on how people see themselves, largely through advertising that promotes narrow and unrealistic beauty standards. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on whether this influence is ultimately harmful.

These days, keeping up with the latest fashion trends can feel like an expensive and never-ending pursuit. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether the fashion industry does more harm than good.

It is generally assumed that fashion is simply a matter of personal expression, when in fact it also drives significant pressure around appearance, spending and environmental waste. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on this issue.

It is common knowledge that fast fashion produces vast quantities of waste, considering how quickly cheaply made clothing is discarded each year. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on the wider effects of the fashion industry.

Over the past few decades, the fashion industry has grown into one of the most powerful and fastest-changing sectors in the world. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on whether that growth has come at too high a cost.

It is generally assumed that fashion trends are harmless fun, as most people simply enjoy dressing well without a second thought. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on why I believe the reality is more complicated.

It is a well-established fact that language learning offers far more than exam success, from the personal enjoyment of mastering something new to genuine career advantages. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on this question.

These days, learning a foreign language is often treated as little more than a box to tick for an exam. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether there is far more to it than that.

It is generally assumed that students only learn languages to pass tests, when in fact many find genuine pleasure and personal growth in the process. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on this issue.

It is common knowledge that speaking more than one language opens doors professionally, considering how many employers now value multilingual skills highly. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on why language learning matters beyond exams.

Over the past few years, language learning has increasingly been valued for the confidence and opportunity it brings, rather than for exam results alone. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on this question.

It is generally assumed that passing a language exam is the main point of studying a language, as this is usually how progress is measured in school. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on why I believe this misses the bigger picture.

It is a well-established fact that understanding a country's history helps explain many of the values and events that shape it today. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on whether such history should be compulsory learning for everyone.

These days, many people question whether studying the past is as important as preparing for the future. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether national history deserves a central place in education.

It is generally assumed that history lessons are of limited practical use, when in fact they can shape both national identity and future decision-making. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on this issue.

It is common knowledge that societies often repeat past mistakes, considering how rarely historical lessons are studied in depth. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on why learning national history matters.
Over the past few decades, there has been ongoing debate about how much weight history should be given in school curriculums. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on this question.

It is generally assumed that focusing on the future is more valuable than studying the past, as the modern world moves so quickly. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on why I believe history remains essential.

These days, most communication takes place through a screen rather than face-to-face, although technology has also made it easier than ever to stay in touch. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of this shift.

Nowadays, smartphones and social media dominate how people interact, in spite of the fact that these same tools help maintain relationships across long distances. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of our growing reliance on technology.

Few people would deny that constant screen use can get in the way of real conversation, despite the fact that technology also allows friendships to survive despite distance and busy schedules. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of modern communication habits.

It is widely recognised that technology has transformed how people build and maintain relationships, although not everyone agrees on whether this change has been for the better. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of this shift.

It is generally assumed that face-to-face interaction is being lost to screens, despite the fact that video calls and messaging keep many long-distance relationships alive. Consequently, it is essential that we study the arguments in favour and against blaming technology for reduced sociability.

Few people would deny that phones and social media have changed the way we socialise, in spite of the fact that these tools have also connected people who would otherwise have lost touch. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of technology's effect on our social lives.

These days, buying almost anything online takes only a few clicks, although this convenience has come at a cost to traditional high street shops. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of online versus in-store shopping.

Nowadays, online retailers offer round-the-clock convenience and often lower prices, in spite of the fact that shoppers can no longer try products before buying them. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of shopping online.

Few people would deny that online shopping saves both time and money, despite the fact that many local shops have struggled to survive as a result. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of this shift in shopping habits.

It is widely recognised that online shopping has transformed the retail industry, although concerns remain about its effect on town centres and local businesses. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of buying online rather than in person.

It is generally assumed that online shopping is simply more convenient, despite the fact that not being able to see or try a product before purchase often leads to disappointment and returns. Consequently, it is essential that we study the arguments in favour and against shopping online.

Few people would deny that online shopping has made consumers' lives easier in many ways, in spite of the fact that it has also placed serious pressure on traditional retailers. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of this ongoing shift.

It is a well-established fact that top athletes earn vast sums of money, largely thanks to sponsorship deals that often exceed their actual sporting salaries. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on whether such earnings are justified.

These days, it is not unusual to hear about footballers or tennis players earning more in a week than most people earn in a year. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether these salaries are fair.

It is generally assumed that high pay simply reflects hard work and talent, when in fact commercial sponsorship plays an enormous role in driving athletes' earnings even higher. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on this issue.

It is common knowledge that professional sport generates huge global revenue, considering how many people worldwide watch major sporting events each year. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether athletes deserve their share of that income.

Over the past few decades, athlete salaries and sponsorship deals have grown to levels that would once have seemed unimaginable. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on whether this growth has gone too far.

It is generally assumed that all professional athletes are overpaid, as headlines tend to focus only on the highest earners in the most popular sports. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on why I believe the picture is more nuanced.

These days, more people than ever are choosing to work for themselves, although traditional employment still offers a level of security that self-employment cannot easily match. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of both options.

Nowadays, self-employment offers freedom over how, when and where people work, in spite of the fact that a steady salary and paid holidays remain out of reach for many freelancers. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of working independently.

Few people would deny that being your own boss brings genuine independence, despite the fact that irregular income can make financial planning far more difficult. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of self-employment versus traditional work.

It is widely recognised that self-employment allows for a more flexible work-life balance, although this flexibility often comes at the cost of financial stability. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of choosing this path.

It is generally assumed that working for yourself is always preferable to working for somebody else, despite the fact that many self-employed people struggle with unpredictable income and long hours. Consequently, it is essential that we study the arguments in favour and against self-employment.

Few people would deny that traditional employment offers valuable security, in spite of the fact that many workers now prize independence and flexibility more highly than a guaranteed salary. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of both types of work.

It is a well-established fact that advertising surrounds almost every aspect of modern life, from television and social media to the packaging on everyday products. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on whether its overall effect on society is positive.
These days, it is almost impossible to avoid advertising in some form throughout the day. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether this constant exposure benefits society.

It is generally assumed that advertising simply helps consumers make informed choices, when in fact it often relies on persuasive techniques that can encourage unnecessary spending. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on this issue.

It is common knowledge that advertising drives competition between companies, considering how businesses constantly try to outdo one another to attract customers. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether this competition benefits society as a whole.

Over the past few decades, advertising has grown from simple print notices into a vast, highly targeted industry. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on whether this growth has been good for society.

It is generally assumed that advertising is a harmless part of modern life, as most people barely notice it during their daily routines. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on why I believe its influence deserves closer scrutiny.

It is a well-established fact that supporting others, whether through friendship, family or volunteering, plays a significant role in building stronger communities. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on whether everyone should make helping others a regular habit.

These days, busy schedules often leave little time to think about helping people outside our immediate circle. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether we should make more effort to do so.

It is generally assumed that helping others is simply a personal choice, when in fact it can bring real benefits both to communities and to the people who help. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on this issue.

It is common knowledge that strong communities depend on people looking out for one another, considering how much local services rely on volunteers and everyday acts of kindness. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on why regular helping matters.

Over the past few years, there has been growing recognition of how much helping others contributes to personal wellbeing, not just to those receiving support. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on this question.

It is generally assumed that helping others is only necessary in times of crisis, as everyday life leaves little obvious opportunity for it. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on why I believe this assumption is mistaken.

It is a well-established fact that school sport teaches skills such as teamwork and discipline that extend well beyond the playing field. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on whether it should be compulsory for every student.

These days, physical education is often one of the more controversial subjects on the school timetable. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether all students should be required to take part.

It is generally assumed that compulsory sport benefits every student equally, when in fact some pupils genuinely dislike physical activity and may struggle to engage with it. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on this issue.

It is common knowledge that regular exercise brings significant health benefits, considering how sedentary many students' lifestyles have become. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether sport should remain a compulsory part of school life.

Over the past few years, concerns about rising inactivity among young people have brought the role of school sport back into focus. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on this question.

It is generally assumed that every student should be forced to take part in sport regardless of their preferences, as the benefits are considered to outweigh any personal reluctance. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on why I believe the picture is more complicated.

It is a well-established fact that children are exposed to a huge volume of advertising every day, much of it designed specifically to appeal to a young audience. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on whether such advertising should be banned.

These days, advertisements aimed at children appear across television, apps and social media almost constantly. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether this kind of marketing should be allowed to continue.

It is generally assumed that children's advertisements are harmless entertainment, when in fact they can place real financial and psychological pressure on young audiences and their families. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on this issue.

It is common knowledge that children can struggle to distinguish advertising from genuine entertainment, considering how persuasive modern marketing techniques have become. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether such adverts should be restricted.

Over the past few years, growing concern about children's wellbeing has brought advertising aimed at young audiences under increasing scrutiny. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on this question.

It is generally assumed that advertisements for children are simply colourful and fun, as this is usually how they are designed to appear. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on why I believe there is a more serious side to consider.

These days, family sizes vary considerably, although each type of household offers a very different upbringing for the children within it. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of large and small families.

Nowadays, larger families offer children the chance to learn valuable social skills from siblings, in spite of the fact that they also face greater financial pressure than smaller households. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of family size.

Few people would deny that smaller families can give each child more individual attention, despite the fact that larger families often provide richer social experiences growing up. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of both family sizes.

It is widely recognised that family size can shape a child's upbringing in lasting ways, although opinions differ on whether larger or smaller families offer the better environment. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of each.

It is generally assumed that larger families are always more crowded and less comfortable, despite the fact that many children from big families value the companionship it brings. Consequently, it is essential that we study the arguments in favour and against different family sizes.

Few people would deny that resources must stretch further in a larger family, in spite of the fact that having several siblings can teach patience and cooperation from an early age. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of large versus small families.

Over the past few years there has been a significant increase in the speed of technological change, since new developments in communication, healthcare and automation continue to emerge at a striking pace. Consequently, it is interesting to predict what may happen to daily life in the years ahead.

There is a growing recognition that technology already shapes almost every part of modern life, given that communication, work and healthcare all depend on it more than ever. Consequently, it is interesting to predict what technology's future impact might be.

Nowadays, few areas of life remain untouched by technology, with artificial intelligence and automation advancing particularly quickly. Therefore, it is vital to look at where these developments might be heading.

Nowadays, breakthroughs in communication and healthcare technology appear almost weekly, with each one raising fresh questions about the future. Consequently, it is interesting to predict what may happen as this pace of change continues.

Few people would deny that technology has already transformed the way we live, with entire industries reshaped by automation and connectivity in just a few decades. Hence, it is worth considering how far this transformation is likely to go.

There is a growing recognition that the coming years are likely to bring major changes to how we work, communicate and manage our health, given how rapidly artificial intelligence is advancing. Therefore, this essay will speculate on the future impact of technology on our lives.

These days, many students choose to take a gap year before starting university, although doing so brings both valuable opportunities and real risks. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of this choice.

Nowadays, a gap year offers the chance to travel, work or volunteer before continuing education, in spite of the fact that taking time away from study can be difficult to reverse. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of taking a gap year.

Few people would deny that a gap year can build valuable independence and life experience, despite the fact that some students struggle to regain their academic momentum afterwards. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of this decision.

It is widely recognised that taking time off before university can be personally rewarding, although the financial cost of travelling or living independently is a significant consideration. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of a gap year.

It is generally assumed that a gap year is always beneficial, despite the fact that it also carries a real risk of losing academic focus and falling behind financially. Consequently, it is essential that we study the arguments in favour and against taking one.

Few people would deny that stepping away from formal education for a year can be a valuable experience, in spite of the fact that it also requires careful financial and academic planning. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of taking a gap year.

There is a growing recognition that childhood obesity has become a serious health issue in many countries, in order to protect the long-term wellbeing of the next generation. Consequently, this essay will suggest ways to address this growing problem.

It is a well-established fact that poor diet and inactivity are the leading causes of childhood obesity, in order to reverse a trend that continues to worsen in many communities. Consequently, this essay will suggest ways to address these underlying causes.

It is often argued that children today are less active and less healthy than previous generations, as a result of changing diets and increasingly sedentary lifestyles. Consequently, this essay will suggest ways to address the resulting rise in childhood obesity.

It is often argued that unhealthy eating habits are difficult to reverse once established, due to the fact that processed and sugary foods remain cheap and widely available. Hence, it is vital to explore practical solutions to help children develop healthier habits.

It is a well-established fact that schools have an important role to play in tackling childhood obesity, due to the fact that they can shape both diet and exercise habits from an early age. Therefore, it is essential to find workable remedies through education and healthier school environments.

There is a growing recognition that reducing childhood obesity will require action on several fronts, owing to the combined effects of poor diet, inactivity and limited health education. Hence, it is vital to explore practical solutions that address all three.

It is common knowledge that many young people struggle to put down their phones, due to the constant notifications that social media platforms are deliberately designed to send. Hence, it is vital to examine the main causes of this growing addiction.

It is a well-established fact that social media use has risen sharply among teenagers, given that platforms are built to keep users engaged for as long as possible. Therefore, it is essential to understand the roots of this behaviour.

There is a growing recognition that social media addiction is becoming more widespread, owing to the constant stream of notifications, likes and messages that apps are designed to generate. Consequently, this essay will explore the causes of this problem and its effects.

These days, near-constant social media use has become the norm for many teenagers, owing to the addictive design of the platforms themselves. Hence, it is vital to examine the main causes behind this pattern of behaviour.

It is a well-established fact that social media addiction is closely linked to declining mental health among young people, given that constant comparison with others online often damages self-esteem. Therefore, it is essential to understand the roots of this connection.

It is often argued that social media addiction has become a serious problem among teenagers, due to the combination of constant notifications and the pressure of comparing themselves to others online. Hence, it is vital to examine the main causes and effects of this issue.

Few people would deny that working from home has grown enormously in popularity in recent years, despite the fact that opinions remain divided on whether it is genuinely better than working in an office. Therefore, this essay will defend the position that remote work offers real advantages overall.

There is a growing recognition that remote work saves employees valuable time and offers greater comfort, while reduced social interaction with colleagues remains a genuine drawback. Hence, in this essay I will explain why I largely agree that working from home is the better option.

There is a growing recognition that home working suits many employees' lifestyles far better than commuting to an office each day, while critics rightly point out the risk of isolation. Therefore, I intend to argue that working from home is, on balance, the more beneficial arrangement.

It is common knowledge that working from home eliminates the daily commute and allows for a more comfortable working environment, while some employees miss the social side of office life. Consequently, I will set out the reasons why I largely support this way of working.

It is often argued that remote work offers unmatched flexibility and comfort, although reduced contact with colleagues is a fair criticism to raise. Therefore, I intend to argue that working from home is, on balance, the better choice for most employees.

Few people would deny that working from home has changed how people balance their time and workload, whereas critics argue that something important is lost when face-to-face contact with colleagues disappears. Hence, in this essay I will explain why I largely agree that its benefits outweigh this drawback.

Over the past few years there has been a significant increase in the number of electric cars on our roads, since concerns about climate change and air pollution continue to grow. Consequently, it is interesting to predict what may happen to transport in the years ahead.

There is a growing recognition that electric vehicles offer a cleaner alternative to petrol and diesel cars, given that many governments now offer incentives to encourage their use. Consequently, it is interesting to predict what may happen as adoption continues to rise.

Nowadays, electric cars are becoming an increasingly common sight on the roads, with battery technology improving rapidly year on year. Therefore, it is vital to look at where this trend might be heading.

Nowadays, more drivers than ever are switching to electric vehicles, with government incentives and environmental concerns both playing a significant role. Consequently, it is interesting to predict what may happen to the car industry as a result.

Few people would deny that electric cars have moved from a niche choice to a mainstream option, with improvements in range and charging technology removing many previous obstacles. Hence, it is worth considering how this trend is likely to develop.

There is a growing recognition that electric vehicles are likely to dominate the roads within a generation, given the combination of environmental pressure, government support and rapidly advancing battery technology. Therefore, this essay will speculate on the future of transport as this trend continues.

These days, online learning has become a mainstream option in education, although it demands a level of self-discipline that traditional classrooms do not always require. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of both approaches.

Nowadays, studying online offers flexibility that traditional classrooms simply cannot match, in spite of the fact that students often miss the face-to-face interaction a physical classroom provides. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of online learning.

Few people would deny that online courses allow students to learn at their own pace, despite the fact that many find it harder to stay motivated without a teacher present in the room. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of this way of studying.

It is widely recognised that online learning has opened up education to people who could not otherwise attend in person, although the lack of direct interaction with teachers and classmates remains a real drawback. Therefore, it is vitally important to consider the advantages and disadvantages of each method.

It is generally assumed that online learning is simply a more convenient version of the classroom, despite the fact that it requires far greater self-motivation to succeed. Consequently, it is essential that we study the arguments in favour and against learning online.

Few people would deny that online learning offers flexibility that suits many students' lives, in spite of the fact that classroom learning still offers valuable interaction that is difficult to replace. Therefore, it is vital that we analyse the pros and cons of both approaches.

It is a well-established fact that pets provide companionship that can significantly reduce feelings of loneliness, particularly among people living alone. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on whether pets genuinely improve mental health.
These days, millions of households include a pet as part of daily family life. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether keeping a pet genuinely benefits people's wellbeing.

It is generally assumed that owning a pet is purely a matter of enjoyment, when in fact caring for an animal can bring real structure, purpose and stress relief to daily life. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on this issue.

It is common knowledge that pets require considerable time, money and responsibility, considering the ongoing costs of food, care and veterinary treatment. Therefore, in this essay I will explain my opinion on whether these demands are outweighed by the benefits pets provide.
Over the past few years, growing scientific interest has focused on the link between pet ownership and improved mental health. Therefore, this essay will make clear where I stand on this question.

It is generally assumed that pets are simply a pleasant addition to family life, as few people consider the deeper psychological benefits they can bring. Consequently, I intend to set out my personal view on why I believe pets play a more significant role in wellbeing than commonly assumed.
